name: Memory / RAG
summary: Agent retrieves relevant documents from a knowledge base before generating a grounded answer.
about: Retrieval-Augmented Generation decouples knowledge from the model weights. Unlike ReAct — where an agent decides opportunistically whether to call a tool — RAG hardwires retrieval into the pipeline: the Retriever always fetches relevant document chunks before the Generator produces a single token. This guarantees grounding in a specific knowledge base, keeps answers traceable to sources, and avoids the model hallucinating facts it was never trained on.
example_prompts: What is our refund policy for annual subscriptions?
How do I set up SSO with Okta?
What are the rate limits on the v2 API?
Which data regions are available for enterprise customers?

retriever.description: Queries the knowledge base and extracts the most relevant chunks for the question
retriever.system: You are a Retriever agent. Call the retrieve tool with a focused query, then output the raw retrieved chunks exactly as returned — do not summarise, reformat, or add commentary. The generator will synthesise them. Stop after one retrieve call.

generator.description: Answers the question using only the retrieved context
generator.system: You are a Generator agent. You will receive the user's question and a block of retrieved document chunks. Answer the question using only the provided context — do not use knowledge outside of it. If the context does not contain enough information, say so explicitly rather than guessing. Cite the source labels (e.g. docs/faq.md) where relevant.

context.description: Retrieved document chunks from the knowledge base
